posting.sponsorName: Joint Apprenticeship and Training Committee for Ironworkers, Local Union #33
posting.locationSummary: Finger Lakes, Southern Tier, and Western regions of New York
posting.applicationSummary: Apply online at ironworkers33.org or through the IW 33 mobile app, or complete an application in person at 650 Trabold Road, Rochester, NY 14624, Monday through Friday, 9:00 am to 3:00 pm, except legal holidays. Applications must be received by February 28, 2027.
posting.applicationHours: Monday through Friday, 9:00 am to 3:00 pm, except legal holidays
posting.applicationFeeDetails: No application fee is stated. If accepted, applicants must join the union and remit within 60 days the Apprenticeship Initiation Fee of $100 plus the first month's dues, currently $33 per month for apprentices.
posting.ageRequirement: Must be at least 18 years old.
posting.educationRequirement: Must have a high school diploma or a high school equivalency diploma such as TASC or GED. Proof is required after selection and before enrollment.
posting.transportationRequirement: Must have a valid New York State driver’s license, be able to drive company vehicles on public streets, and have reliable transportation to job sites and required classes. Must arrive on time to work and school.
posting.drugTestRequirement: Must pass a drug test, at the sponsor’s expense, after selection and before enrollment.
posting.classRequirement: Must attend required classes at the approved school as part of the apprenticeship.
allRequirements: Must be at least 18 years old.
Must have a high school diploma or a high school equivalency diploma such as TASC or GED. Proof will be required after selection and prior to enrollment in the apprenticeship program.

Must sign an affidavit stating that they are physically able to do the work of an Ironworker (outside), including working at heights, on or off ladders, with materials and tools; walking, climbing, bending, reaching, lifting, and carrying; working a full day of strenuous physical labor; working on building erection jobs, bridges, open wall reinforcing jobs, ornamental work, sheeting jobs, rigging jobs, and welding structural jobs; constant bending and reaching while connecting, bolting up, rigging, placing, and tying reinforcing bars, placing metal sheeting and decking on buildings, putting up metal window sashes or stair railings, and setting up welding equipment; lifting and carrying heavy steel; and understanding there is no light work in the Ironworker trade.
Must pass a drug test, at the expense of the sponsor, after selection and prior to enrollment in the apprenticeship program.
Must have a valid New York State driver’s license. Apprentices will be required to drive company vehicles on public streets.
Must provide a copy of a New York State driver’s license and Social Security card if accepted into the apprenticeship program.
Must have reliable transportation to and from various job sites and required classes at the approved school. Must arrive to the job and to school on time.
Must be willing to work overtime when required.
Must join the union and remit within 60 days the Apprenticeship Initiation Fee, which is $100, plus the first month’s dues, currently $33 per month for apprentices.
posting.requirementsSummary: Applicants must be at least 18, have a high school diploma or equivalency, pass a drug test, have a valid NYS driver’s license and reliable transportation, and be physically able to perform strenuous ironworker duties.
posting.contactSummary: For further information, contact Ironworkers Local Union #33 at (585) 288-2630.
